DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT HINKLEY & GORDON STRUCTURAL PEST CONTROL CO. INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES FROM PLAINTIFF CLAUDIA W. BRISSON - DEFENDANT HINKLEY & GORDON STRUCTURAL PEST CONTROL CO., INC. HAS MOVED THE COURT TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES FROM PLAINTIFF CLAUDIA W. BRISSON. IN DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOVING PAPERS DEFENDANT HAS MOVED TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES. IN THE MOVING PAPERS AND IN THE DECLARATION OF JOHN P. NAHRA DEFENDANTS HAVE REQUESTED THAT THE PLAINTIFF BE COMPELLED TO PROVIDE RESPONSES TO "REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, SET NO. 1, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET NO. 1 AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION, SET NO. 1" ALL OF WHICH WERE SAID TO BE ATTACHED AS EXHIBITS "A" THROUGH "C". DEFENDANT HAS ATTACHED AS EXHIBITS "A" THROUGH "C" SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET NO. 1, REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION, SET NO. 1, AND FORM INTERROGATORIES. THERE HAS BEEN NO OPPOSITION FILED BY PLAINTIFF CLAUDIA W. BRISSON. THE DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET NO. 1, AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION, SET NO. 1 IS GRANTED AND THE PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE VERIFIED RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THE COURT ORDER. THE DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, SET NO. 1 IS DENIED AS THE MOTION DOES NOT INCLUDE THE REQUEST FOR ADMISSION OR PROOF OF SERVICE OF SAME. THE DEFENDANT DID NOT MOVE THE COURT TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND SO THAT ISSUE IS NOT BEFORE THE COURT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE PRO-TEM: DIANNE D. PEEBLES; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Form Interrogatories, Set One, Responses From Plaintiff Claudia W. Brisson; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Attorney Daniel Patrick Leonard of Baker Keener & Nahra LLP (213-241-0900 ex. 999) CourtCall appearance for Defendant hinkley & Gordon Structural Pest Control Co.,Inc. moving party. No other appearances.

The Court adopted tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT HINKLEY & GORDON STRUCTURAL PEST CONTROL CO. INc.'s motion to compel Plaintiff Claudia Brisson's Responses to Form Interrogatories without objection is GRANTED. The Plaintiff is ordered to submit verified Responses within 20 days of this order. The request for sanctions is DENIED. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Dianne Peebles, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

Defendant Hinkley & Gordon Structural Pest Control Co., Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff Claudia W. Brisson
Off Calendar Sep-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN P. NAHRA OF THE BAKER, KEENET & NAHRA LLP, TEL. NO. 1-213-241-0900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HINKLEY & GORDON STRUCTURAL PEST CONTROL CO. INC.'s Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff Claudia W. Brisson -
Defendant Hinkley & Gordon Structural Pest Control Co., Inc. has moved the court to Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff Claudia W. Brisson. In Defendant's Notice of Motion and moving papers Defendant has moved to compel Discovery Responses. In the moving papers and in the Declaration of John P. Nahra Defendants have requested that the Plaintiff be compelled to provide responses to "Requests For Admission, Set No. 1, Special Interrogatories, Set No. 1 and Request for Production, Set No. 1" all of which were said to be attached as Exhibits "A" through "C". Defendant has attached as Exhibits "A" through "C" Special Interrogatories, Set No. 1, Request for Production, Set No. 1, and Form Interrogatories. There has been no opposition filed by Plaintiff Claudia W. Brisson. The Defendant's Motion to Compel is granted in part and denied in part. The Defendant's motion to compel Plaintiff's Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set No. 1, and Request for Production, Set No. 1 is granted and the Plaintiff is ordered to provide verified responses, without objection, within 30 days of the court order. The Defendant's Motion to compel responses to Requests For Admission, Set No. 1 is denied as the motion does not include the Request for Admission or proof of service of same. The Defendant did not move the court to compel responses to Form Interrogatories and so that issue is not before the court. Prevailing party to prepare form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro-Tem: Dianne D. Peebles; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/jpt
